review_title: Save time and money and learn as you go!
description: My overall experience has been great. Limitations were due to a misunderstanding on my part of what Replit should and should not be used for. The agent is great at talking through solutions and bouncing ideas off of. I love that it comes up with color themes because I don't like that stuff. It provides you with something to 'react to' that you can then work with the agent to refine.
author: Gabe H.
pros: It's fairly easy to get started. With what I'm doing between Replit and AWS, it would easily cost me thousands upon thousands to pay professional devs to build my app for me. For most people that are probably building something simple this is an amazing tool.
cons: It's over-confident sometimes, like in migrating to AWS. It doesn't 'remember' it's a dev environment. There's no warning that you're approaching context window limitations. There's a temptation to stick with an agent for additional build out of a feature rather than opening up a new chat. This is really like nitpicky stuff though. The human user still has to have an understanding of how Replit is doing what it's doing. A little learning goes a long way.
author_profession: CEO, CRO, CFO, CMO, CPO, CTO
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: How Replit Made Me a Faster, More Confident Builder
description: Replit's advantages clearly outweigh its drawbacks. It has significantly improved my development process, making me a more efficient and productive builder through its streamlined IDE, collaborative features, accessibility, and broad language support. Ultimately, Replit has enhanced my technical skills and fostered a more dynamic approach to coding.
author: Steven C.
pros: One of the key advantages of this system is its ability to facilitate instant project starts and rapid application deployments. This means that users can quickly launch new projects and share them with others, significantly streamlining workflows and accelerating development cycles. The ease of getting started and deploying applications contributes to a highly efficient and collaborative environment.
cons: However, a notable limitation of the system is its lack of offline access. A constant and stable internet connection is mandatory for its operation, which renders it impractical for use in environments where internet connectivity is unreliable or nonexistent. This dependency on an online connection makes the system unsuitable for fieldwork, travel, or any scenario where continuous internet access cannot be guaranteed, thereby restricting its utility for users who require the flexibility of working offline.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: My experience with Replit AI has been extremely frustrating and damaging to my business
description: My experience with Replit AI has been extremely frustrating and damaging to my business. What initially seemed like a promising development platform quickly turned into an expensive and unreliable service. The pricing model feels misleading because the AI often requires multiple repeated prompts just to complete simple tasks, and every prompt counts toward usage. This can quickly drive up costs and lead to a very high bill for work that should be simple. Recently, my app was suddenly taken down with no warning or clear explanation, which immediately disrupted my business operations. Shortly after that, I started receiving thousands of emails in my Gmail inbox within minutes, completely overwhelming my email and making it difficult to communicate with clients for several days. Customer support has been almost non-existent. I reached out multiple times but received slow and vague responses that did not resolve the issue. Meanwhile, the platform still appears to be attempting to charge my account for activity connected to these problems. After transferring my business to this platform, the result has been downtime, billing confusion, and a lack of real support. I plan to report the issue and share my experience so other business owners are aware before relying on this platform. Let us start a class action lawsuit on this business. Report this to your governor office as well and the business bureau
author: Al P.
pros: The illusion that it was my solution for my business to stop wasting money on freelancers. I switch from Wix to Replit. Boy this is becoming a regret
cons: Over charging my accounts Scam Receiving 1000 email spam from the A.I running up my bill. How horrible the customer service is and was. (Trash) Took down all my business sites for 3 day still not up and then trying to charge my account.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Good to start with, but costs add up quickly
description: Overall, it’s been a positive experience for getting things up and running quickly. It’s a great tool for prototyping, experimenting, and collaborating without needing a complex setup. However, over time you do start to notice the cost side more, especially if you’re using it regularly or for larger projects. It’s a platform I’d still use, but with a clearer understanding of how pricing scales as usage increases.
author: Martina S.
pros: . You can go from idea to working prototype without much setup, which makes it great for testing concepts or learning. The interface is clean and user-friendly, and collaboration features make it easy to work with others in real time. It also supports a wide range of languages and tools, which gives you flexibility depending on what you’re trying to build.
cons: The main drawback is the pricing once you move beyond basic usage. While it’s accessible at the start, costs can add up quickly when you rely on it more heavily or need additional resources. This can make it less suitable if you’re working within a strict budget. There’s also a feeling that some limitations only become clear once you’re already invested, which can be frustrating when planning longer-term use.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Turning Ideas into Apps with Speed and Simplicity
description: can now quickly turn my thoughts and ideas into working apps. I'm constantly evaluating processes and systems, both at work and in everyday life, to come up with automated web apps that streamline tasks and solve real problems. It's changed the way I approach problem-solving.
author: Carl B.
pros: Replit makes it incredibly easy to turn ideas into MVPs—fast. I’ve always had lots of app ideas, but building them used to feel either too expensive to outsource or too complex to do on my own. Replit has completely changed that. It’s opened up a world of exciting possibilities and made development accessible in a way I didn’t think was possible.
cons: The monthly token model can be confusing at times. I’ve found myself going over my allotted tokens because the Agent will often auto-build solutions when I’m just looking for feedback or suggestions on how to approach a development problem. It would be helpful if there were a way to clarify intent more easily to avoid unnecessary token usage.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Fantastic with a caveat
description: Very good overall if you take into account the flaws inherent in AI. Does a good job if you explicitly say what you want most of the time. Just have to be explicit and verbose with instructions.
author: Jesse B.
pros: It allows someone with technical experience to make a working prototype without writing any code. It's very fast.
cons: It's too eager to implement solutions with a low success rate. If I ask it a question, often it implements a bogus solution that I didn't ask for. Also, their customer service is all AI as far as I can tell, so if you have a real issue, good luck with that
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: I Wish They'd Invested in Support
description: TL;DR: Replit underindexed on creating a robust support infrastructure, to the detriment of its users and its lone support rep. First, I was excited to use it and impressed with the initial output. Then I needed some customization, at which point I fell into the absolute hellhole of Replit AI support. It took way, way too long to get the agent to understand what I wanted to build, all while burning through credits (and I was collaborating with other people and multiple LLMs to see if I could engineer the prompts better, but no matter what, it was completely confused). Finally, I gave up and went to support, which was also AI. While very personable and seemingly competent, it told me conflicting information about what I could and could not do on the website I was building. This was also over multiple days, and again, causing me to burn through credits. Then I escalated to a human, which took the human 8 days to respond to, at which point they said they were very overwhelmed. They did apologize and issue a refund, which I really appreciated. I abandoned it for Squarespace. Which is sad, because trust me, I would have loved for Replit to work - but it just wasn't there yet for what I needed.
author: Kate R.
pros: - beautiful, efficient and accurate design - easy to use (at first) - great idea until you need customization
cons: - absolute hellhole of AI enablement and support, all which burn up your credits and force you to either abandon the project or upgrade.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: My favorite vibe coding tool
description: Created several solutions of which I use one on a daily basis. I am not a programmer, still I can create solutions. Of course it will help that I am an IT manager and therefore are kind of tech savvy.
author: Jaap M.
pros: The best I tested so far (also tested Cursor, Loveable and Base44). It's easy, almost always create solutions for ideas and errors. Great user interface.
cons: You have to pay for Replit, but I also had to five an OpenAI API key when I asked some heavier tasks to fulfill (rating and translating). So I pay twice, but for now it's worth it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: From Zero to App in No Time—Just Code
description: I enjoy using Replit, especially when I need to get a frontend up and running quickly. I also really appreciate it when dealing with bugs. Sometimes, after an app is finished or halfway done, I share it with others as users so they can try it out. This way, they can use my application without me having to worry about reverse proxies, ports, Docker, or other deployment complexities.
author: Mico W.
pros: I don’t need to prepare much for the initial setup when creating an application. Everything can be done through prompts alone, eliminating the usual time-consuming tasks that add no real value. Even fixing problems or bugs that arise later can be handled quickly in the same streamlined way.
cons: Every month, I nearly reach—or completely use up—the quota provided. The good part is that I’m making full use of it, but it also means it can run out.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Really poor product and customer support
description: Awful and really poor customer support. They were unresponsive and never resolved my issue. I gave up and had to leave all my work behind.
author: Ronan K.
pros: Initially I liked the ease of the interface. It was intuitive and it was creating some good results, so I continued
cons: When I had invested hours (and run out of credits). I didn't care about the cost, it was the work. It deleted all of my work and could not recover it. The irony is that it cost me every attempt it made to recover my work. But was not successful.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Love Replit, transformed my work flows.
description: Great product overall. A real game changer for non-code professionals to be able to create what they're looking for. A massive short cut for passing on to pro developers, building internal tools (where it doesn't necessarily matter if everything works totally) and if you're looking to delve into the world of software development for clients etc then it's a fantastic start and makes it a lot more user friendly and comprehensive than other solutions out there.
author: Paul T.
pros: Superb product. I'm not a software developer so this has been magnificent as a tool to help me create proof of concepts and explore ideas. I started doing this for my own business - a piece of software to support a regular activity, totally bespoke to me, where other off the shelf products require lots of customisation. It's grown beyond that to developing for others and creating just unique ideas which I'd hope to take to market.
cons: There is a decent learning curve required. Not to get started but to think like a software developer. For example, if you ask for a 'My Account' area - you'll get one with lots of common options - superb! However, you'll need to think and define how each part of this works. Exactly how password reset functions should work etc. This is my experience, you must be familiar with using SaaS software to get the most out of this. Otherwise you'll get a visual solution but not necessarily a totally productive one. Lots of work needs to go into the prompt and lots and lots of testing.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Replit is my favorite tool
description: I've been a fan of Replit since I first heard about it on a podcast. I've basically learned everything I know about coding with Python, software development, the AI stack, and everything that goes along with putting together a modern web application. Having everything right there and easily deployable is a huge plus. Being able to access it from anywhere is also a huge plus. You haven't lived until you've made a few code changes and re-deployed your app on your phone while walking the dog!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Replit has become my happy place, I tell everyone about it. It's intuitive, fun to use, and POWERFUL. Whether using the Agent or just taking advantage of the cloud based, one-click IDE or easy deployments, it's become my favorite web tool of the past few years. Being able to spin up a prototype for an idea, no matter how big or small, is amazing.
cons: There's not a lot I don't like about Replit. The new effort-based pricing is opaque and unpredictable, which isn't great. But at the same time, the Agent is super powerful, and of all the Agentic AI coding assistants I've used, Replit's is by far the best at setting everything up. It's tight integration with the IDE is second to none, and I feel like it gives Claude Code a run for its money when setting up a new project.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Replit AI Agent Review
description: It’s been great in helping me code what I want. I’d even call it somewhat magical. It can’t do everything for you, but with patience and the right prompts, you can build very cool things in a surprisingly short amount of time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The ability to build apps with only modest coding skills, while AI accelerates error detection and speeds up the entire development process.
cons: The AI agent performs well across many topics, but when it comes to Replit deployments or databases, it sometimes struggles with basic tasks. For example, it may fail to create a database when asked, or misunderstand questions about deployment issues and configurations.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Honest review about Replit
description: I think that the overall experience with Replit is very good. Although there are some things that I don't like, I think that overall Replit is a very good and amazing app. I would recommend it to everyone whether you are a novice or a expert.
author: Minh D.
pros: I think that the Replit app has a very good overall AI system. It also has a lot of accessibility and easy to reach. I also see that the coding system about the integration is one of the best I have ever seen
cons: The most apparent weakness in the app is about launching apps. To be more specific, it is about the PyQt 5 library app. It sometimes can't launch, and I think that it is very annoying to not launch normally with my code being correct. Also the AI can sometimes can code incorrectly and the most apperent of all, it can be very annoying to just
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Worth it for the big and little guy
description: I love using Replit as I have zero coding experience. It allows me to chip away at my project and saves me a lot of time, effort and money! What I’ve built in three months would have cost me $300,000 and taken 18 months via the traditional route.
author: Blair G.
pros: I love how easy it is to have my ideas created right in front of my eyes. I can then use the tools available to tweak my project as needed and debug as I go along. It does require more input that you’d assume but that way you get to know the nitty gritty of your project.
cons: Sometimes, rarely, the agent doesn’t get it right and will miss key parts when implementing my request. To be expected when I’m building a very complex project.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Excellent prompt based coding tool
description: Overall, it has been a great experience and almost flawless. The deployment and other things are great too.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Simple interface to enter prompt and get started on building apps, and how replit asks clarifying details before getting started with building the apps.
cons: The frequent changes to charging. Checkpoint based to effort based now. The current one is good, but I feel sometimes the effort/pricing does not match and consistent. Also, bugs introduced by additional effort and efforts to fix them are counted as additional issue. Some validation and line based effort around bug fix related changes must be priced differently.
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: Despite some frustrations, I am very pleased and sticking with Replit
description: Despite the Cons, I am sticking with Replit for a while. Typically replit can complete a task in less time than it traditionally took me as a product owner and project manager just to define a developer task/user story in Jira.
author: Matt B.
pros: The initial fast prototyping and UI design out of the gate. Saved months and tens of thousands of dollars
cons: 1. Replit makes too many assumptions and does things without explicit permission. 2. I repeatedly ask replit to add to the project .md file to make sure it follows guidlines, but it frequently does not stay within those guidelines 3. As I get into more complicated procedures, the replit agent goes in circles trying to solve a problem, and I ultimately have to roll back, wasting a lot of my time, and wasting money spent with the Replit Agent Service
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Fastest way I’ve ever built an app — amazing for rapid prototyping
description: Replit has quickly become one of my favorite pieces of software ever. It makes building, testing, and deploying apps incredibly fast, and it’s perfect for experimenting with new ideas on the fly. While there are small areas that could be improved, the speed, flexibility, and ease of use make it an absolute standout in my toolkit.
author: Kevin M.
pros: The first time I used Replit, I built a fully functional app in just 5–10 minutes. It’s incredible for rapid prototyping and makes creating web apps fast and intuitive. The interface is clean, and the ability to collaborate and iterate quickly is unmatched.
cons: Occasionally, I run into challenges with image placement, and error handling can sometimes get stuck in loops that are tricky to break. That said, rephrasing my prompts usually gets me past these issues.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Almost great for an underrated product
description: It’s a good product that could be made great if fixes to the environment and UI design would make it great.
author: Carlos S.
pros: The idea that with natural language Replit can take care not only of the development (coding) but also the setup of the environment, hosting etc.
cons: Have not been able to launch a single product because of environment issues. Also that there is not a simple editor to modify simple UI elements. Everything must be done by message with the Ai this very simply things costing money.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Happy Replit User. Looking forward to see what else I can create.
description: I really enjoy developing in Replit. It has already taught me so much about development. I use Replit in conjunction with Claude and Cursor for full-stack development. I deploy all apps on Replit because it is easy and performs well.
author: Kevin V.
pros: The AI Agent is a great tool to easily spin up new ideas into working apps quickly. Once you get into the groove, it is incredible to see what you can create.
cons: The new AI usage credit system is confusing and seems arbitrary. I get that heavier tasks consume more credits, but there could be more transparency.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Replacing teams of developers to quickly launch tools and products!
description: Overall I am very excited about all the opportunities Replit gives me to develop concepts very quickly and to put products and tools into production without needing to hire or contract a single developer. I am able to leverage my background in product development and product management to write up details scopes of work and within minutes have a functional tool ready to go.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Replit started out as a prototyping tool for me to quickly develop a proof of concept for a critical app that was needed for the Non profit organization I work with. Not only was I able to get from concept to working product extremly quickly but the prototype proved itself so well that we are using it as our production product! We are now pursuing other opportunities and ideas to meet our product development needs as we scale.
cons: I found the amount of rework to be significantly high. Many features and changes I ask for create issues or break other working features. I do find myself spending more time correcting broken features than working on new features and testing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: This WiIll Change The World
description: Amazed! A side-line project that has been languishing for 5 years was deployed after just 3 weeks of part time work.
author: Mike P.
pros: Ease of us. Quick to learn. Responsive support. Amazing product. Speed of development and ability to deploy something new extremely quickly.
cons: Agent can sometimes struggle with complex concepts. It's all about providing sufficient detail in the prompt so that it doesn't guess.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Beware, CLIs Are Eating Your Lunch
description: The initial experience was great, but I’m finding myself using Claude Code CLI more often since it’s tailored to my preferences. As a software engineer with over 30 years of experience, I greatly value the flexibility and control that CLI tools offer.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I love most about Replit is how it lets me build SPAs and web apps entirely in the browser—no setup hassle, no environment friction. The collaborative coding, AI-powered assistance, fast deployment, and ready-to-go templates all make development seamless, flexible, and enjoyable
cons: The user interface feels outdated, and the support experience has been disappointing—one of my tickets remained unresolved for over a week. To ensure the service remained online, I ultimately deployed it using Vercel.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Wonderful for SME businesses! But make sure you scope out what you want before using!
description: Good, but I’ll be happier when/if I can deploy out of creation stage, lots of time wasted helping the system debug itself
author: Lorraine E.
pros: I didn’t learn coding, but in using Replit I could finally craft the framework for a proprietary system for my company based on all the elements I believe we need and potential others.
cons: Replit on app creation isn’t great at debugging, it takes far too long to debug and it seems like it only builds on command to your direction, ideally the system should be better at debugging and it shouldn’t need as much interaction to do so, Another disadvantage albeit a positive too! Is that I find it hard to stop and deploy to test, with the endless capabilities you tend to run into rabbit holes of possibilities resulting in losing site of the core purpose of the project, Replit agents could do better and ask more clarifying questions during the development of the systems
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great value for money and very easy to use for app/site building.
description: Overall, great experience with Replit. I hope for some bug fixing, the platform can charge less for these types of tasks, but overall, very cost-effective and user-friendly way to build apps and websites.
author: Nanzhu Z.
pros: Value for the money first as it charges based on the work done for each task of coding with a transparent cost calculation. I like the freedom of designing the website the way I wanted without the need to sticking to a specific template.
cons: The AI agent sometimes takes longer time to understand the natural language and some bug fix takes multiple iterations in order to find the root cause and fix it permanently.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
